- Otherwise: just create the client without link

post.description:
Reset trainer/client profile for clean onboarding testing.

DEV/TEST ONLY: This endpoint is only available in development or
testing environments. In production, this endpoint returns 403.

This endpoint:
- Soft-deletes existing trainer/client profiles for the given email
- Clears their user_id link
- Allows clean onboarding tests when recreating accounts

Use case: When testing onboarding flows, delete and recreate accounts
with the same email to get fresh profiles.

post.description:
Normalize intensity from various methods to 0-1 scale.

Supports:
- Aerobic: RPE, %FCmáx, %vVO₂máx, Zonas (Z1-Z5)
- Strength: RIR, %1RM, RPE
- Anaerobic: RPE, %Vmax, Zonas (Z6-Z7)

Calculate strength load (fuerza).

Converts RIR/%1RM/RPE to normalized intensity, then:
carga_fuerza = volumen_relativo × intensidad_normalizada × 100

Calculate aerobic load using TRIMP-like model.

carga = volumen_relativo * intensidad_normalizada * k_zona (scaled 0-100)

Calculate anaerobic load with density and zone multipliers.

carga = volumen_relativo * intensidad_normalizada**1.2 * k_densidad * k_zona
(scaled 0-100)

Daily Internal Load (CID) from levels 1-10 and k_fase.

cid = ((Vn * In) ** p) * k_fase * 100, clamped to 0-100
Bulk CID calculation for a consecutive set of days starting at start_date.
Aggregate CID per ISO week starting from start_date.

Aggregate CID per calendar month from start_date over items length.

Optionally applies phase weight (w_fase) to monthly totals.
Formula from document: Carga mensual = Σ (CargaSemanal × w_fase)
Master total load CT = sum(partial * weight).

Check for threshold violations in daily metrics.

Detects:
- Daily CID > daily_threshold (default 80)
- Weekly CID sum > weekly_threshold (default 450)
- Consecutive days with CID > consecutive_threshold (default 70)

If create_alerts=True, also creates FatigueAlert records in the database.
Requires client_id and trainer_id when create_alerts=True.
